name: Automobile Engineering
description: Automobile Engineering focuses on the design, manufacturing, and maintenance of automotive vehicles. The curriculum covers vehicle dynamics, powertrain systems, electrical systems, and emerging hybrid technologies.

subjects.name: Automotive Engines
subjects.description: Study of internal combustion engine principles including spark ignition and compression ignition engines. Covers engine components, cycles, fuel systems, cooling, lubrication, and performance characteristics.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the working principles of SI and CI engines
Evaluate engine performance parameters and characteristics
Identify and describe engine components and subsystems
Compare different engine types and fuel systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Engine technology is the core of automotive powertrain design, critical for OEMs and Tier-1 suppliers.

subjects.name: Vehicle Dynamics
subjects.description: Study of forces and moments acting on a vehicle during motion. Covers ride comfort, handling, stability, tire dynamics, and suspension behavior.
learning_outcomes: Analyze vehicle ride and handling characteristics
Model tire forces and vehicle response to inputs
Evaluate suspension system design parameters
Assess vehicle stability under various driving conditions
subjects.industry_relevance: Vehicle dynamics is fundamental to chassis tuning and active safety systems in modern automobiles.

subjects.name: Automotive Transmission
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of manual and automatic transmission systems including gearboxes, clutches, torque converters, and CVTs. Covers drivetrain components and power flow analysis.
learning_outcomes: Design and analyze manual and automatic transmission systems
Evaluate torque converter and CVT performance characteristics
Calculate gear ratios and power flow in drivetrains
Troubleshoot common transmission faults
subjects.industry_relevance: Transmission systems are key to vehicle efficiency and performance, with growing demand for EV transmission engineers.

subjects.name: Chassis Engineering
subjects.description: Design and analysis of vehicle chassis frames, suspension systems, steering mechanisms, and braking systems. Covers structural analysis, material selection, and manufacturing processes.
learning_outcomes: Design chassis frames for different vehicle types
Analyze suspension geometry and steering mechanisms
Evaluate braking system performance and design parameters
Perform structural analysis of chassis components
subjects.industry_relevance: Chassis engineering is central to vehicle safety, weight reduction, and structural performance in automotive manufacturing.

subjects.name: Automotive Electrical Systems
subjects.description: Study of electrical and electronic systems in automobiles including batteries, alternators, starting systems, lighting, and wiring harnesses. Covers modern CAN bus and automotive networking protocols.
learning_outcomes: Design automotive electrical circuits and wiring systems
Analyze battery charging and starting system performance
Implement CAN bus communication protocols
Troubleshoot electrical faults in vehicle systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Modern vehicles increasingly rely on complex electrical architectures, driving demand for automotive electrical engineers.

subjects.name: Vehicle Design
subjects.description: Principles of automotive vehicle design including body styling, ergonomics, crashworthiness, and structural optimization. Covers CAD modeling, design for manufacturing, and regulatory standards.
learning_outcomes: Apply design principles to vehicle body and structure
Perform crashworthiness analysis and safety design
Use CAD tools for vehicle component modeling
Evaluate ergonomic factors in vehicle interior design
subjects.industry_relevance: Vehicle design integrates aesthetics, safety, and manufacturability, essential for automotive OEMs and design studios.

subjects.name: Engine Management Systems
subjects.description: Electronic control of internal combustion engines including ECU architecture, fuel injection control, ignition timing, and emission control strategies. Covers OBD diagnostics and calibration techniques.
learning_outcomes: Design and program engine control unit strategies
Implement fuel injection and ignition control algorithms
Diagnose engine faults using OBD systems
Calibrate engine management parameters for performance and emissions
subjects.industry_relevance: Engine management is critical for meeting emission norms and optimizing fuel efficiency in modern vehicles.

subjects.name: Automotive Pollution Control
subjects.description: Study of emission formation mechanisms in IC engines and after-treatment systems including catalytic converters, particulate filters, and SCR systems. Covers emission norms and testing procedures.
learning_outcomes: Identify emission formation mechanisms in IC engines
Design after-treatment systems for emission reduction
Evaluate vehicle compliance with emission regulations
Analyze alternative fuel impacts on emissions
subjects.industry_relevance: Stringent global emission norms (BS-VI, Euro 7) make pollution control expertise critical for automotive compliance.

subjects.name: Vehicle Maintenance
subjects.description: Practical aspects of vehicle maintenance including preventive maintenance schedules, fault diagnosis, engine overhaul, and repair techniques. Covers service procedures for all major vehicle systems.
learning_outcomes: Develop preventive maintenance schedules for vehicles
Diagnose and repair engine, transmission, and chassis faults
Perform system-level troubleshooting using diagnostic tools
Apply safety procedures in workshop environments
subjects.industry_relevance: Vehicle maintenance skills are essential for service centers, fleet management, and aftermarket automotive sectors.

subjects.name: Aerodynamics of Vehicles
subjects.description: Study of aerodynamic forces acting on vehicles including drag, lift, and side forces. Covers wind tunnel testing, CFD simulation, and design optimization for reduced drag and improved stability.
learning_outcomes: Analyze aerodynamic forces and their effect on vehicle performance
Conduct CFD simulations for external vehicle flow
Design aerodynamic body shapes for drag reduction
Interpret wind tunnel test data for design optimization
subjects.industry_relevance: Aerodynamics directly impacts fuel efficiency, high-speed stability, and EV range, making it vital for modern vehicle development.

subjects.name: Hybrid Vehicle Technology
subjects.description: Study of hybrid electric vehicle architectures including series, parallel, and series-parallel configurations. Covers energy storage systems, power electronics, regenerative braking, and energy management strategies.
learning_outcomes: Design hybrid vehicle powertrain architectures
Implement energy management strategies for optimal efficiency
Analyze regenerative braking and energy storage systems
Evaluate hybrid vehicle performance and emissions trade-offs
subjects.industry_relevance: Hybrid technology bridges conventional and electric vehicles, with major OEMs investing heavily in hybrid platforms.

subjects.name: Automotive Materials
subjects.description: Study of materials used in automotive manufacturing including steels, aluminum alloys, composites, polymers, and advanced high-strength materials. Covers material selection, testing, and failure analysis.
learning_outcomes: Select appropriate materials for automotive components
Evaluate material properties through mechanical testing
Analyze failure modes in automotive materials
Compare lightweight materials for vehicle weight reduction
subjects.industry_relevance: Material innovation drives weight reduction, safety, and cost efficiency in automotive manufacturing.

subjects.name: CAD for Automotive
subjects.description: Computer-aided design and modeling techniques specific to automotive applications. Covers 3D solid modeling, surface modeling, assembly design, and drafting using industry-standard CAD software.
learning_outcomes: Create 3D solid and surface models of automotive components
Develop assembly models and generate engineering drawings
Apply GD&T principles to automotive part design
Use CAD software for design validation and simulation
subjects.industry_relevance: CAD proficiency is a fundamental requirement for design roles across the automotive industry.

subjects.name: Autotronics
subjects.description: Integration of electronics and software in automotive systems including engine control, body control modules, infotainment, and advanced driver assistance systems. Covers automotive sensors, actuators, and embedded control.
learning_outcomes: Design automotive electronic control modules
Implement embedded software for vehicle control systems
Integrate sensors and actuators with automotive ECUs
Develop diagnostic protocols for electronic systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Autotronics is the backbone of modern vehicle electronics, with growing demand for connected and autonomous vehicle technologies.

subjects.name: Two & Three Wheelers
subjects.description: Specialized study of two-wheeler and three-wheeler vehicle design, including motorcycle dynamics, scooter transmissions, and auto-rickshaw powertrains. Covers braking, suspension, and stability aspects unique to these vehicles.
learning_outcomes: Analyze motorcycle dynamics and stability characteristics
Design transmission and braking systems for two-wheelers
Evaluate three-wheeler chassis and suspension design
Compare different two-wheeler engine configurations
subjects.industry_relevance: Two and three-wheeler markets dominate in Asia and emerging economies, offering vast employment opportunities.

subjects.name: Automotive Safety
subjects.description: Study of active and passive safety systems in vehicles including airbags, ABS, ESC, collision avoidance, and occupant protection. Covers safety regulations, crash testing, and NCAP ratings.
learning_outcomes: Design active and passive safety systems for vehicles
Analyze crash test data and occupant protection mechanisms
Evaluate vehicle safety ratings and regulatory compliance
Implement ADAS features for collision avoidance
subjects.industry_relevance: Vehicle safety is a top priority globally, with NCAP ratings driving consumer choices and regulatory mandates.
